channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 9 months into ceasefire, areas of Gaza are threatened by Israel's expanding control
item.description: The ceasefire left Gaza split in half: Israel's military occupied the east, and 2 million Palestinians squeezed into the remaining areas. But Israeli forces have been pushing deeper into Gaza.
item.content:encoded: The ceasefire left Gaza split in half: Israel's military occupied the east, and 2 million Palestinians squeezed into the remaining areas. But Israeli forces have been pushing deeper into Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Why these Jewish college students are learning Yiddish
item.description: Young Jewish-Americans have been looking for new ways to engage with Jewish culture. Some have found community by learning Yiddish, a language with roots in 10th century Europe.
item.content:encoded: Young Jewish-Americans have been looking for new ways to engage with Jewish culture. Some have found community by learning Yiddish, a language with roots in 10th century Europe.
item.dc:creator: Nirvani Williams

item.title: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh isn't as focused on employment, part of the dual mandate
item.description: The Federal Reserve has two main goals: price stability and maximum employment. But new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh seems to be leaning into price stability and away from full employment as equal goals.
item.content:encoded: The Federal Reserve has two main goals: price stability and maximum employment. But new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh seems to be leaning into price stability and away from full employment as equal goals.
item.dc:creator: Wailin Wong

item.title: Farmers say it's time to expand the H-2A visa for seasonal agriculture labor
item.description: Amid an administration crackdown on various forms of legal migration, there's one type of visa that even many Republicans support: the popular H-2A program for seasonal agricultural workers.
item.content:encoded: Amid an administration crackdown on various forms of legal migration, there's one type of visa that even many Republicans support: the popular H-2A program for seasonal agricultural workers.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: PM churnover has some Britons wondering what has made the U.K. so ungovernable
item.description: The 2008 financial crisis and Brexit shrank the UK economy and led to a revolving door of PMs. Analysts say the first-past-the-post parliamentary system is ill-suited to modern, multi-party politics.
item.content:encoded: The 2008 financial crisis and Brexit shrank the UK economy and led to a revolving door of PMs. Analysts say the first-past-the-post parliamentary system is ill-suited to modern, multi-party politics.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Microsoft is doing a hard reset of its XBOX gaming division
item.description: This week, Microsoft announced they were laying off over 3,000 staff at Xbox. What will the impact be on the company? And what do these layoffs say about the gaming industry right now?
item.content:encoded: This week, Microsoft announced they were laying off over 3,000 staff at Xbox. What will the impact be on the company? And what do these layoffs say about the gaming industry right now?
item.dc:creator: Monica Nickelsburg

item.title: Former U.S. envoy reacts to Iran strikes and NATO summit
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to NATO Nicholas Burns regarding this week's developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict -- and the NATO summit this week.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to NATO Nicholas Burns regarding this week's developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict -- and the NATO summit this week.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: Questions over AI campaign parodies rise as regulations struggle to control it
item.description: The regulation of AI use in Michigan and other states may not make a difference in what people see, raising questions over whether AI campaign parodies are political satire or something darker.
item.content:encoded: The regulation of AI use in Michigan and other states may not make a difference in what people see, raising questions over whether AI campaign parodies are political satire or something darker.
item.dc:creator: Colin Jackson

item.title: A Florida airport is officially renamed for Trump. What does he stand to gain?
item.description: Florida's Palm Beach International Airport will adopt a new name Thursday to honor President Trump. He'll be the first president to have an airport named after him while in office.
item.content:encoded: Florida's Palm Beach International Airport will adopt a new name Thursday to honor President Trump. He'll be the first president to have an airport named after him while in office.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: With Platner announcement he's out of Senate race, Maine Democrats weigh what to do now
item.description: Graham Platner built a grassroots movement in Maine. As Maine Democrats weigh what's next now that he's exited the race, winning that support will be key.
item.content:encoded: Graham Platner built a grassroots movement in Maine. As Maine Democrats weigh what's next now that he's exited the race, winning that support will be key.
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller
